name: Baños de Montemayor: Vía Verde and Calzada Romana Loop
name_original: Baños de Montemayor – circular por la Vía Verde de la Plata y Calzada Romana
description: Hike the Baños de Montemayor: Vía Verde and Calzada Romana Loop, a moderate 3.0-mile route through historical paths and scenic valleys.
region_intro: Hiking around Baños De Montemayor offers access to a diverse landscape within the Ambroz Valley, characterized by mountainous terrain ranging from 850 to 1,200 meters. The region features abundant chestnut and oak forests, natural water sources, and the scenic Baños de Montemayor Reservoir. These physical features provide varied backdrops for outdoor activities, from valley paths to higher elevation trails. Best hiking trails around Baños De Montemayor The most popular hiking route is Baños de Montemayor: Vía Verde and Calzada Romana Loop, a 3.0 miles (4.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 31 minutes to complete. This moderate route combines historical paths with scenic valleys. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Muela from La Garganta through El Puertecito, a difficult 6.2 miles (10.0 km) path. This trail features significant elevation gain, suitable for those seeking a more demanding experience. Local hikers also love the Waterfall and Stone Aqueduct – La Chorrera Waterfall loop from Hervás, a 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail leading through natural waterfalls, often completed in about 4 hours 6 minutes. Hiking around Baños De Montemayor is defined by mountainous terrain, dense chestnut and oak forests, and the Baños de Montemayor Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to challenging climbs. The routes in Baños De Montemayor are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 115 reviews. More than 950 hikers have used komoot to explore Baños De Montemayor's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Baños de Montemayor?
A: Baños de Montemayor offers a wide selection of hiking opportunities, with over 75 trails available for exploration. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy strolls to more challenging climbs through the mountainous terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Baños de Montemayor?
A: The Ambroz Valley, where Baños de Montemayor is located, benefits from a mild climate year-round, making it suitable for hiking in most seasons. While trails are accessible throughout the year, some higher peaks like Pinajarro can be snow-capped in winter, offering a different experience. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures and vibrant scenery.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Baños de Montemayor?
A: Yes, Baños de Montemayor has 16 easy hiking trails, perfect for beginners or families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990854911/banos-de-montemayor-via-verde-and-calzada-romana-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños de Montemayor: Vía Verde and Calzada Romana Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 4.9 km route that combines historical paths with scenic valleys, offering an accessible experience for many.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in the area?
A: For those seeking a more demanding experience, Baños de Montemayor offers 14 difficult hiking trails. A notable challenging route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990846597/la-muela-from-la-garganta-through-el-puertecito" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Muela from La Garganta through El Puertecito&lt;/a&gt;, a 10 km path featuring significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Baños de Montemayor?
A: Yes, the area features several circular hiking routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990854911/banos-de-montemayor-via-verde-and-calzada-romana-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños de Montemayor: Vía Verde and Calzada Romana Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular trail. Additionally, the Baños de Montemayor Reservoir offers a 12.2 km circular route (PR-CC 40) with stunning views, suitable for all walkers.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, you can explore trails that lead to natural waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17315261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall and Stone Aqueduct – La Chorrera Waterfall loop from Hervás&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.1-mile (13 km) trail that showcases the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5701178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Chorrera Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see along the trails?
A: Hikers in Baños de Montemayor will encounter diverse natural features, including lush forests of chestnut and oak trees, the scenic Baños de Montemayor Reservoir, and natural pools with crystalline waters. The mountainous terrain also offers panoramic views, with peaks like Pinajarro visible, sometimes snow-capped in winter.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or unique points of interest along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can explore parts of the ancient Roman road, the Calzada Romana, which is incorporated into routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990854911/banos-de-montemayor-via-verde-and-calzada-romana-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde and Calzada Romana Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Another unique feature is the illuminated Puerto de Béjar Tunnel, part of an old railway line, which can be experienced on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17315360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Béjar Tunnel loop from Puerto de Béjar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Baños de Montemayor?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 115 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscape, which includes mountainous terrain, dense chestnut and oak forests, and the scenic Baños de Montemayor Reservoir, offering varied backdrops for outdoor activities.

Q: Are the trails in Baños de Montemayor dog-friendly?
A: Many trails in Baños de Montemayor are suitable for hiking with dogs, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to check specific trail regulations before you go.

Q: Is there public transport available to access hiking trailheads?
A: While Baños de Montemayor is a charming village, public transport options directly to all trailheads might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to access the starting points of various hikes. The Vía Verde de la Plata, an old railway line, is generally accessible and often runs through or near towns.

Q: Are there places to eat or relax after a hike in Baños de Montemayor?
A: Yes, Baños de Montemayor offers amenities for post-hike relaxation. The town is known for its 2,000-year-old Roman thermal baths, providing a unique way to unwind. You can also find local restaurants and cafes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/987118" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pequeña Estación Bar Restaurant Café&lt;/a&gt;, to refuel after your outdoor adventures.

Going up the Roman road we find the Mirador de la Cruz.
Pleasant place to have breakfast at one of the tables in front of the restaurant.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Baños de Montemayor: Vía Verde and Calzada Romana Loop?
faqs.answer: This loop is classified as moderate. The Vía Verde sections are generally flat and easy, as they follow a former railway line. However, parts of the Calzada Romana can be more uneven due to stone or cobbled paths, requiring some sure-footedness. The ascents are progressive and manageable for most fitness levels.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this hike?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a varied terrain. The Vía Verde de la Plata sections are typically compact dirt or gravel. The Calzada Romana portions may feature stone or cobbled paths. The loop also includes paved and unpaved tracks, gravel paths, and some asphalt sections.

faqs.question: What are the main sights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers scenic views of the Ambroz Valley, oak forests, and spectacular mountain vistas. You'll also pass through the Puerto de Béjar Tunnel and see the Cruz del Humilladero. There are excellent viewpoints providing panoramic scenes of Baños de Montemayor and its reservoir.

faqs.question: Is the Baños de Montemayor: Vía Verde and Calzada Romana Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. Dogs are usually welcome on Vías Verdes and similar paths, but it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other hikers.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to hike this loop. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The best times to hike are generally spring and autumn, when the weather is mild and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summers can be hot, and winters might be chilly, though the trail is accessible year-round.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Vía Verde and Calzada Romana Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Baños de Montemayor, as the starting point, typically offers public parking options within the town or near the Vía Verde access points. Look for designated parking areas upon arrival.

faqs.question: Does this loop connect with other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route integrates with several other paths. It forms a crucial part of the longer Vía de la Plata hiking - 02 Merida to Salamanca and the Vía Verde de la Plata (Casas del Monte - Béjar). You'll also find sections that overlap with the Camino de Arrieros and other parts of the Calzada Romana.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and varied terrain, the Vía Verde sections are very family-friendly. The Calzada Romana parts might be a bit more challenging for very young children due to uneven surfaces, but overall, it's a rewarding experience for active families.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, Baños de Montemayor is a town known for its thermal baths and offers various amenities. You can find restaurants like Restaurante Magnolia, cafes, pubs, and accommodation options in the town, making it a convenient base for your hike.

faqs.question: What should I be aware of regarding the trail surface?
faqs.answer: While much of the Vía Verde is well-maintained, some reforested areas may have hidden holes, so watch your step. The Calzada Romana sections can be uneven with stones and cobbles. Sturdy footwear is recommended for comfort and safety on these varied surfaces.
